sections.paragraph:
to cleave (kindred roots are גָּזַע, גָּדַע, קָצַע, to these answer the Sanscr. tshid, to cut, Gr. σχίζω, Germ. fcheiden), שָׁסַע שֶׁסַע פַּרְסָה Lev 11:7 and פְּרָסֹות שׁ׳ שׁ׳ Lev 11:3 Deu 14:6 to cleave the cleft of the hoof, i.e. to be cloven-hoofed. Compare הִפְרִיס.

Piel.-

(1) to cleave, Lev 1:17.

(2) to rend, to tear in pieces a lion, Jud. 14:6 .

(3) metaph. to rend with words, i.e. to chide, to upbraid, 1Sa 24:8.
